d3s_e1_a.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e1_a.text: Day 3 and cash is tight. You need to make smart financial decisions today or the business could be in real trouble. Every peso counts.

d3s_e1_b.speaker: You
d3s_e1_b.text: Bills are piling up and revenue is lower than expected. I need to cut costs somewhere without destroying what makes this place special.

choices.text: Cut ingredient quality slightly to save costs
Launch flash sale to generate quick cash
Apply for a small business emergency loan

d3s_e1_c_staff.speaker: Staff
d3s_e1_c_staff.text: Reduced hours means reduced pay. We understand the business is struggling but this puts real pressure on us personally.

d3s_e1_c_quality.speaker: Regular Customer
d3s_e1_c_quality.text: Something tastes different today. Not as good as usual. I hope standards are not dropping here.

d3s_e1_c_sale.speaker: Customer
d3s_e1_c_sale.text: A flash sale! Perfect timing. I brought three friends along. You should do these more often!

d3s_e1_c_loan.speaker: Bank Officer
d3s_e1_c_loan.text: Given your short operating history the loan comes with higher interest. But the funds are available within 24 hours if you need them.

d3s_e1_result_staff.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e1_result_staff.text: Cost cutting through labor is a double edged sword. It preserves cash but damages the team trust that carries you through hard times.

d3s_e1_result_quality.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e1_result_quality.text: Quality cuts are visible to loyal customers immediately. Short term savings can trigger long term reputation damage that costs far more.

d3s_e1_result_sale.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e1_result_sale.text: Flash sales generate immediate cash flow and foot traffic. Creative revenue generation beats cost cutting when protecting culture matters.

d3s_e1_result_loan.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e1_result_loan.text: Debt financing buys time but adds future pressure. Use borrowed capital only for revenue generating activities not just covering operating costs.

d3s_e2_a.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e2_a.text: A difficult conversation is needed. Your accountant has reviewed the books and the numbers are concerning. You need a plan today.

d3s_e2_b.speaker: Accountant
d3s_e2_b.text: At the current burn rate you have about three weeks of runway left. You need to either increase revenue by 30% or cut costs by 20%. What is your plan?

choices.text: Do both — moderate cuts and moderate growth push
Seek a business mentor or advisor for help

d3s_e2_c_revenue.speaker: Accountant
d3s_e2_c_revenue.text: Aggressive revenue focus is high risk high reward. If it works you come out stronger. If it does not you burn through what little runway you have left.

d3s_e2_c_costs.speaker: Accountant
d3s_e2_c_costs.text: Cost cutting extends runway but does not solve the fundamental revenue problem. Make sure you are not cutting things that generate income.

d3s_e2_c_both.speaker: Accountant
d3s_e2_c_both.text: A balanced approach is pragmatic. Neither fully solves the problem but together they buy you meaningful time to find a sustainable solution.

d3s_e2_c_mentor.speaker: Business Mentor
d3s_e2_c_mentor.text: Smart move seeking help! I have seen this situation before. Three specific changes can turn this around within two weeks. Let me show you.

d3s_e2_result_revenue.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e2_result_revenue.text: Betting on growth during a cash crisis takes courage. It can work but requires laser focus and flawless execution under pressure.

d3s_e2_result_costs.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e2_result_costs.text: Cost control without revenue growth is a slow decline. It buys time — use that time to build revenue not just to survive.

d3s_e2_result_both.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e2_result_both.text: Balance under pressure is mature financial thinking. You are not solving everything but you are moving in the right direction on multiple fronts.

d3s_e2_result_mentor.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e2_result_mentor.text: Knowing when to ask for help is one of the most underrated business skills. Pride kills more businesses than bad markets do.

d3s_e3_a.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e3_a.text: Despite the financial pressure your team has been incredible. End of day and you need to decide how to keep morale alive without spending much.

d3s_e3_b.speaker: Staff
d3s_e3_b.text: We know things are tight boss. We are still here and still giving our best. We just want to know you see us and appreciate what we are doing.

choices.text: Promise better times ahead with a specific plan
Offer a small gesture — team meal on you
Be honest about how serious the situation is

d3s_e3_c_gratitude.speaker: Staff
d3s_e3_c_gratitude.text: That genuinely means a lot. Sometimes just being seen and acknowledged is enough to keep going. We are with you through this.

d3s_e3_c_promise.speaker: Staff
d3s_e3_c_promise.text: A specific plan gives us something to hold on to. We can handle hard times when we know there is a real path forward.

d3s_e3_c_meal.speaker: Staff
d3s_e3_c_meal.text: A team meal during tough times! It is such a small thing but it means so much. You put us first even when money is tight.

d3s_e3_c_honest.speaker: Staff
d3s_e3_c_honest.text: We appreciate you being straight with us. It is scary to hear but we would rather know the truth than be kept in the dark.

d3s_e3_result_gratitude.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e3_result_gratitude.text: Authentic appreciation costs nothing but means everything. The best leaders make their people feel valued especially when resources are scarce.

d3s_e3_result_promise.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e3_result_promise.text: Hope with a plan is leadership. Hope without a plan is just wishful thinking. Your team needs both the vision and the roadmap.

d3s_e3_result_meal.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e3_result_meal.text: Small gestures during hard times have disproportionate impact. The memory of being cared for in difficulty outlasts any bonus given in good times.

d3s_e3_result_honest.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e3_result_honest.text: Radical transparency builds deep trust. Teams that face hard truths together develop resilience that carries them through any crisis.
